edges.description: Defective transport and renal reabsorption cause urinary carnitine wasting and low plasma/tissue carnitine.
Renal tubular OCTN2 dysfunction causes urinary carnitine wasting.
Defective OCTN2-mediated carnitine transport and renal reabsorption produces low circulating free carnitine.
Low intracellular carnitine limits mitochondrial import of long-chain fatty acids for beta-oxidation.
Systemic carnitine depletion is reflected by markedly reduced plasma free carnitine.

edges.target: Cardiac remodeling and fibrosis in OCTN2 deficiency

edges.description: Impaired fatty acid oxidation in skeletal muscle contributes to the myopathic presentation.
Energy deficiency in skeletal muscle contributes to hypotonia.
Skeletal muscle energy failure and myopathy can increase creatine kinase.
Cardiomyocyte contractile dysfunction and fibrosis produce the prevalent dilated cardiomyopathy phenotype.
edges.source: Cardiac remodeling and fibrosis in OCTN2 deficiency

edges.description: PCD cardiomyopathy can also present as a hypertrophic cardiomyopathy phenocopy.
edges.source: Cardiac remodeling and fibrosis in OCTN2 deficiency

edges.description: Severe cardiomyopathy and metabolic disturbance can culminate in sudden death or arrest.
edges.source: Cardiac remodeling and fibrosis in OCTN2 deficiency

edges.description: Cardiac energy failure and remodeling in CTD/PCD can produce life-threatening ventricular arrhythmias.
edges.source: Cardiac remodeling and fibrosis in OCTN2 deficiency

edges.description: Impaired hepatic fatty acid oxidation and ketogenesis produces hypoketotic hypoglycemia during fasting.
Acute metabolic decompensation includes decreased blood glucose during fasting stress.
Acute hepatic metabolic disruption contributes to hyperammonemia.
Hyperammonemia is the biochemical manifestation of increased ammonia during decompensation.
Hyperammonemia and hepatic energy failure contribute to metabolic encephalopathy.
Acute encephalopathy can present with lethargy or decreased alertness.
Life-threatening ventricular arrhythmias can progress to sudden cardiac arrest or death.
Low plasma free carnitine reports failed OCTN2-mediated carnitine uptake and renal reabsorption.
Free carnitine (C0) is the primary circulating readout of systemic carnitine depletion in PCD.
Increased urinary carnitine excretion reports defective proximal-tubule OCTN2 reabsorption.
Urinary carnitine wasting explains and tracks the systemic carnitine depletion state.
Elevated ammonia reports acute hepatic metabolic decompensation during fasting or stress.
Blood ammonia is the direct biochemical readout of the hyperammonemia phenotype.
Low blood glucose reports failure of hepatic energy production during decompensation.
Blood glucose is the direct biochemical readout of hypoglycemia.
Elevated creatine kinase tracks muscle injury in the myopathic presentation.
Creatine kinase elevation is a downstream laboratory readout of muscle energy failure during PCD decompensation.

nodes.description: Administration of acetyl-L-carnitine in patients with hepatic encephalopathy associated with carnitine deficiency can improve mental and psychological conditions, representing an adjunctive therapeutic approach.

evidence.reference_title: The role of genetic defects in carnitine-associated hepatic encephalopathy: a review of literature.
evidence.snippet:
the administration of acetyl-L-carnitine in patients with HE can improve 
their mental and psychological conditions.

nodes.description: Emergency supportive care during metabolic crises includes intravenous glucose infusion to reverse catabolism, correction of metabolic acidosis, treatment of hyperammonemia, and intravenous L-carnitine. Prompt intervention prevents progression to encephalopathy and organ failure.

evidence.reference_title: A Rare Treatable Cause of Cardiomyopathy: Primary Carnitine Deficiency.
evidence.snippet:
Carnitine deficiency can result in 
acute metabolic decompensation or, in a more insidious presentation, 
cardiomyopathy.

evidence.reference_title: The role of genetic defects in carnitine-associated hepatic encephalopathy: a review of literature.
evidence.snippet:
If a deficiency of carnitine is 
identified through clinical symptoms or laboratory results in patients with 
liver dysfunction, treatment with carnitine replacement therapy is recommended.

evidence.reference_title: The role of genetic defects in carnitine-associated hepatic encephalopathy: a review of literature.
evidence.snippet:
A strong correlation exists between the insufficiency 
of carnitine and the occurrence of HE.

evidence.reference_title: Inborn Errors of Metabolism with Myopathy: Defects of Fatty Acid Oxidation and the Carnitine Shuttle System.
evidence.snippet: During episodes of metabolic decompensation, glucose and ketone bodies are inappropriately low. Transaminases and ammonia may be moderately elevated, and metabolic acidosis, prolonged prothrombin time, and elevated creatine kinase (CK) can occur.

readouts.interpretation: Elevated ammonia reports acute hepatic metabolic decompensation during fasting or stress.
Blood ammonia is the direct biochemical readout of the hyperammonemia phenotype.

evidence.reference_title: A Rare Treatable Cause of Cardiomyopathy: Primary Carnitine Deficiency.
evidence.snippet:
Carnitine deficiency can result in 
acute metabolic decompensation or, in a more insidious presentation, 
cardiomyopathy.

evidence.reference_title: Inborn Errors of Metabolism with Myopathy: Defects of Fatty Acid Oxidation and the Carnitine Shuttle System.
evidence.snippet: During episodes of metabolic decompensation, glucose and ketone bodies are inappropriately low.

readouts.interpretation: Low blood glucose reports failure of hepatic energy production during decompensation.
Blood glucose is the direct biochemical readout of hypoglycemia.

nodes.description: Sudden cardiac death or arrest can occur in untreated PCD or with discontinuation of L-carnitine supplementation. Arrhythmias secondary to cardiomyopathy contribute to this risk.

evidence.reference_title: Inborn Errors of Metabolism with Myopathy: Defects of Fatty Acid Oxidation and the Carnitine Shuttle System.
evidence.snippet: Life-threatening arrhythmias can also occur, including nonsustained ventricular tachycardia with periods of sinus rhythm and ventricular premature beats, even in the presence of only borderline left ventricular hypertrophy.

nodes.description: Standard heart failure therapy including ACE inhibitors, beta-blockers, and diuretics may be required for patients presenting with cardiomyopathy and heart failure. Cardiac monitoring with echocardiography is essential for long-term follow-up. ICD placement may be considered for arrhythmia management.

evidence.reference_title: A Rare Treatable Cause of Cardiomyopathy: Primary Carnitine Deficiency.
evidence.snippet:
Cardiomyopathy associated with PCD often presents with 
life-threatening heart failure.

nodes.description: Chronic carnitine depletion in cardiomyocytes suppresses contractile and calcium-handling gene programs, triggers fibroblast expansion, and activates pro-fibrotic macrophage signaling. Single-nucleus RNA-seq studies of OCTN2-deficient hearts reveal downregulation of contractile genes (MYH7, TNNI3, TNNT2, RYR2), increased fibroblast abundance, elevated GAS6-AXL signaling initiating fibrosis through EMT, and SPP1-positive macrophage-driven fibroblast activation.
nodes.id: Cardiac remodeling and fibrosis in OCTN2 deficiency

evidence.reference_title: Unraveling cardiomyocyte responses and intercellular communication alterations in primary carnitine deficiency cardiomyopathy via single-nucleus RNA sequencing.
evidence.snippet:
OCTN2-deficient cardiomyocytes displayed transcriptomic alterations 
indicative of reduced contractility, developmental abnormalities, and fibrosis.

evidence.reference_title: A Rare Treatable Cause of Cardiomyopathy: Primary Carnitine Deficiency.
evidence.snippet:
This presentation also usually includes skeletal 
muscle myopathy.

evidence.reference_title: Inborn Errors of Metabolism with Myopathy: Defects of Fatty Acid Oxidation and the Carnitine Shuttle System.
evidence.snippet: During episodes of metabolic decompensation, glucose and ketone bodies are inappropriately low. Transaminases and ammonia may be moderately elevated, and metabolic acidosis, prolonged prothrombin time, and elevated creatine kinase (CK) can occur.

readouts.interpretation: Elevated creatine kinase tracks muscle injury in the myopathic presentation.
Creatine kinase elevation is a downstream laboratory readout of muscle energy failure during PCD decompensation.

nodes.description: Avoidance of prolonged fasting is essential to prevent metabolic decompensation. Frequent feeding schedules and carbohydrate-rich snacks before bed help maintain energy supply and reduce dependence on fatty acid oxidation.

evidence.reference_title: Nutritional Management of Patients with Fatty Acid Oxidation Disorders.
evidence.snippet:
Treatment of fatty acid oxidation disorders is based on dietary, pharmacological 
and metabolic decompensation measures.

evidence.reference_title: A Rare Treatable Cause of Cardiomyopathy: Primary Carnitine Deficiency.
evidence.snippet:
Carnitine deficiency can result in 
acute metabolic decompensation or, in a more insidious presentation, 
cardiomyopathy.

nodes.description: Dilated cardiomyopathy is the most common cardiac manifestation of PCD, presenting with reduced left ventricular ejection fraction and heart failure. It is often the presenting feature and can be reversed with L-carnitine supplementation.

evidence.reference_title: A Rare Treatable Cause of Cardiomyopathy: Primary Carnitine Deficiency.
evidence.snippet:
Cardiomyopathy associated with PCD often presents with 
life-threatening heart failure.

evidence.reference_title: A Rare Treatable Cause of Cardiomyopathy: Primary Carnitine Deficiency.
evidence.snippet:
Herein, we present a 10-month-old male patient with PCD, 
which was diagnosed while investigating the etiology of dilated cardiomyopathy 
and confirmed by molecular genetic analysis.

nodes.description: Metabolic encephalopathy occurs during acute decompensation episodes, presenting with altered consciousness ranging from excessive sleepiness to coma.

evidence.reference_title: The role of genetic defects in carnitine-associated hepatic encephalopathy: a review of literature.
evidence.snippet:
A strong correlation exists between the insufficiency 
of carnitine and the occurrence of HE.

evidence.reference_title: Incorporating Next-Generation Sequencing as a Second-Tier Test for Primary Carnitine Deficiency.
evidence.snippet:
Newborns with free carnitine (C0) levels below 
8.5 μmol/L were selected for second-tier genetic testing.

readouts.interpretation: Low plasma free carnitine reports failed OCTN2-mediated carnitine uptake and renal reabsorption.
Free carnitine (C0) is the primary circulating readout of systemic carnitine depletion in PCD.

nodes.description: In the liver, carnitine deficiency impairs fatty acid oxidation and ketogenesis, leading to hypoketotic hypoglycemia during fasting. Accumulation of toxic metabolites contributes to hyperammonemia and hepatic encephalopathy, particularly in the infantile presentation of PCD.

evidence.reference_title: The role of genetic defects in carnitine-associated hepatic encephalopathy: a review of literature.
evidence.snippet:
Carnitine acts as a vital component in 
facilitating the transport of long-chain fatty acids into the mitochondria, 
thereby enabling their oxidation for the generation of energy. Carnitine 
additionally assumes a crucial role in the functionality of the brain. Carnitine 
deficiency is associated with various types of inherited disorders related to 
low levels of carnitine.

evidence.reference_title: A Rare Treatable Cause of Cardiomyopathy: Primary Carnitine Deficiency.
evidence.snippet:
Carnitine deficiency can result in 
acute metabolic decompensation or, in a more insidious presentation, 
cardiomyopathy.

evidence.reference_title: Inborn Errors of Metabolism with Myopathy: Defects of Fatty Acid Oxidation and the Carnitine Shuttle System.
evidence.snippet: Loss of carnitine in the kidney results in very low concentration in other tissues, resulting in severe impairment of long-chain FAO, which leads to hypoketotic hypoglycemia with fasting and stress.

evidence.reference_title: Inborn Errors of Metabolism with Myopathy: Defects of Fatty Acid Oxidation and the Carnitine Shuttle System.
evidence.snippet: During episodes of metabolic decompensation, glucose and ketone bodies are inappropriately low. Transaminases and ammonia may be moderately elevated, and metabolic acidosis, prolonged prothrombin time, and elevated creatine kinase (CK) can occur.

nodes.description: Secondary hyperammonemia occurs during acute metabolic decompensation, contributing to encephalopathy. The mechanism involves impaired urea cycle function secondary to metabolic disruption.

evidence.reference_title: The role of genetic defects in carnitine-associated hepatic encephalopathy: a review of literature.
evidence.snippet:
A strong correlation exists between the insufficiency 
of carnitine and the occurrence of HE.

evidence.snippet:

nodes.description: PCD can mimic hypertrophic cardiomyopathy and may be mistakenly attributed to sarcomeric protein dysfunction. Recognition of this phenocopy is important for appropriate management.

evidence.reference_title: Unmasking Primary Carnitine Deficiency as a Mimic of Hypertrophic Cardiomyopathy.
evidence.snippet:
Primary carnitine deficiency may mimic hypertrophic cardiomyopathy and be 
mistakenly attributed to genotype-negative sarcomeric protein dysfunction in 
hypertrophic cardiomyopathy.

evidence.reference_title: A novel pathogenic variant in the carnitine transporter gene, SLC22A5, in association with metabolic carnitine deficiency and cardiomyopathy features.
evidence.snippet:
Clinical evaluations, echocardiography, and cardiac magnetic resonance 
imaging findings revealed hypertrophic cardiomyopathy as a clinical presentation 
of PCD.

nodes.description: Fasting intolerance with impaired ketogenesis and hypoglycemia is a hallmark of acute metabolic decompensation in PCD, especially in infants during intercurrent illness.

evidence.reference_title: A Rare Treatable Cause of Cardiomyopathy: Primary Carnitine Deficiency.
evidence.snippet:
Carnitine deficiency can result in 
acute metabolic decompensation or, in a more insidious presentation, 
cardiomyopathy.

evidence.reference_title: Inborn Errors of Metabolism with Myopathy: Defects of Fatty Acid Oxidation and the Carnitine Shuttle System.
evidence.snippet: Loss of carnitine in the kidney results in very low concentration in other tissues, resulting in severe impairment of long-chain FAO, which leads to hypoketotic hypoglycemia with fasting and stress.

evidence.reference_title: The role of genetic defects in carnitine-associated hepatic encephalopathy: a review of literature.
evidence.snippet:
Carnitine 
deficiency is associated with various types of inherited disorders related to 
low levels of carnitine.

nodes.description: Intracellular carnitine depletion impairs the carnitine shuttle system, reducing transfer of long-chain fatty acids into mitochondria for beta-oxidation. This causes energy failure particularly in tissues with high fatty acid oxidation demand such as heart and skeletal muscle, and drives metabolic decompensation during fasting or catabolic stress.

evidence.reference_title: A Rare Treatable Cause of Cardiomyopathy: Primary Carnitine Deficiency.
evidence.snippet:
Carnitine deficiency can result in 
acute metabolic decompensation or, in a more insidious presentation, 
cardiomyopathy.

evidence.reference_title: Inborn Errors of Metabolism with Myopathy: Defects of Fatty Acid Oxidation and the Carnitine Shuttle System.
evidence.snippet: Importantly, carnitine is necessary for import of long-chain fatty acyls-CoAs into mitochondria as acylcarnitines for FAO.

nodes.description: Lifelong oral L-carnitine replacement is the disease-modifying cornerstone of PCD management. Standard dosing is 100-300 mg/kg/day in divided doses, titrated to plasma free carnitine levels and clinical response. Early treatment can reverse cardiomyopathy, with LVEF normalization reported within months.

evidence.reference_title: A Rare Treatable Cause of Cardiomyopathy: Primary Carnitine Deficiency.
evidence.snippet:
Early recognition of this disorder and treatment with carnitine 
can avoid life-threatening complications related to cardiomyopathy.

nodes.description: Decreased alertness and lethargy occur during acute metabolic decompensation and may progress to encephalopathy.

evidence.reference_title: A novel pathogenic variant in the carnitine transporter gene, SLC22A5, in association with metabolic carnitine deficiency and cardiomyopathy features.
evidence.snippet:
We herein describe an 8-year-old boy with symptoms of weakness and 
lethargy diagnosed with PCD through clinical evaluations, lab tests, 
echocardiography, and cardiac magnetic resonance imaging.

nodes.description: Hypotonia is a common neuromuscular finding in PCD, related to impaired energy metabolism in skeletal muscle.

evidence.reference_title: A Rare Treatable Cause of Cardiomyopathy: Primary Carnitine Deficiency.
evidence.snippet:
This presentation also usually includes skeletal 
muscle myopathy.

evidence.reference_title: Inborn Errors of Metabolism with Myopathy: Defects of Fatty Acid Oxidation and the Carnitine Shuttle System.
evidence.snippet: Later-onset disorders can present with milder skeletal muscle manifestations, including hypotonia, myopathy, and exercise intolerance.

evidence.reference_title: The Human OCTN Sub-Family: Gene and Protein Structure, Expression, and Regulation.
evidence.snippet:
A 
plethora of missense variants with uncertain clinical significance are reported 
both in the dbSNP and the Catalogue of Somatic Mutations in Cancer (COSMIC) 
databases for both genes.

evidence.reference_title: SLC22A5 / systemic primary carnitine deficiency disease (Definitive)
evidence.snippet: SLC22A5 | HGNC:10969 | systemic primary carnitine deficiency disease | MONDO:0008919 | AR | Definitive

nodes.description: The most common pathogenic variant in Chinese PCD patients, accounting for approximately 45% of alleles. Regional variation exists with lower frequency in southern China.
Second most common variant in Chinese PCD, accounting for approximately 26% of alleles.
Nonsense variant accounting for approximately 14% of alleles in Chinese PCD. Homozygous c.760C>T carriers are more likely to present with cardiomyopathy.

evidence.reference_title: Primary carnitine deficiency: Estimation of prevalence in Chinese population and insights into newborn screening.
evidence.snippet:
patients 
carrying homozygous c.760C>T and c.844C>T were more likely to present 
cardiomyopathy, whereas those carrying homozygous c.1400C>G were more likely to 
be asymptomatic

evidence.reference_title: Incorporating Next-Generation Sequencing as a Second-Tier Test for Primary Carnitine Deficiency.
evidence.snippet:
the three most common variants were 
c.760C>T (p.Arg254*), c.51C>G (p.Phe17Leu), and c.1400C>G (p.Ser467Cys).

nodes.description: Novel nonsense variant identified in an Iranian family, associated with hypertrophic cardiomyopathy. Docking analysis demonstrated reduced carnitine binding affinity.

evidence.reference_title: A novel pathogenic variant in the carnitine transporter gene, SLC22A5, in association with metabolic carnitine deficiency and cardiomyopathy features.
evidence.snippet:
Whole-exome sequencing identified a new homozygous variant, SLC22A5 
(NM_003060.4), c.821G > A: p.Trp274Ter, associated with carnitine transport.

nodes.description: Loss-of-function variants in SLC22A5 impair OCTN2-mediated transmembrane carnitine transport in kidney and other tissues.

evidence.reference_title: Screening primary carnitine deficiency in 10 million Chinese newborns: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
evidence.snippet:
Primary carnitine deficiency (PCD) is a rare autosomal recessive 
fatty acid oxidation disorder caused by variants in SLC22A5, with its prevalence 
and SLC22A5 gene mutation spectrum varying across races and regions.

nodes.description: Progressive skeletal muscle weakness and hypotonia result from energy deficiency in oxidative muscle fibers. Myopathy may accompany the cardiomyopathy phenotype.

evidence.reference_title: A Rare Treatable Cause of Cardiomyopathy: Primary Carnitine Deficiency.
evidence.snippet:
This presentation also usually includes skeletal 
muscle myopathy.

evidence.reference_title: Inborn Errors of Metabolism with Myopathy: Defects of Fatty Acid Oxidation and the Carnitine Shuttle System.
evidence.snippet: Later-onset disorders can present with milder skeletal muscle manifestations, including hypotonia, myopathy, and exercise intolerance.

nodes.description: Defective renal tubular reabsorption causes urinary carnitine wasting, producing low plasma free carnitine and tissue carnitine depletion. This is the proximal biochemical consequence driving downstream metabolic failure.

evidence.reference_title: A Rare Treatable Cause of Cardiomyopathy: Primary Carnitine Deficiency.
evidence.snippet:
Carnitine deficiency can result in 
acute metabolic decompensation or, in a more insidious presentation, 
cardiomyopathy.

evidence.reference_title: Inborn Errors of Metabolism with Myopathy: Defects of Fatty Acid Oxidation and the Carnitine Shuttle System.
evidence.snippet: As a result of its deficiency, carnitine is not reabsorbed in the kidney, leading to urinary loss and depletion of blood and tissue levels.

evidence.reference_title: Inborn Errors of Metabolism with Myopathy: Defects of Fatty Acid Oxidation and the Carnitine Shuttle System.
evidence.snippet: CTD deficiency should be suspected by the finding of very low free plasma carnitine concentrations (<10 μmol/L) accompanied by increased fractional excretion of carnitine in urine.

readouts.interpretation: Increased urinary carnitine excretion reports defective proximal-tubule OCTN2 reabsorption.
Urinary carnitine wasting explains and tracks the systemic carnitine depletion state.

nodes.description: Life-threatening ventricular arrhythmias, including nonsustained ventricular tachycardia and ventricular premature beats, can occur in CTD/PCD and may contribute to sudden cardiac death risk.

evidence.reference_title: Inborn Errors of Metabolism with Myopathy: Defects of Fatty Acid Oxidation and the Carnitine Shuttle System.
evidence.snippet: Life-threatening arrhythmias can also occur, including nonsustained ventricular tachycardia with periods of sinus rhythm and ventricular premature beats, even in the presence of only borderline left ventricular hypertrophy.
